transcript: Speaker A: Could I get a sparkling water and do you have any snacks that aren't peanuts? Speaker B: Of course, pretzels okay? Speaker A: Perfect, thanks. Speaker C: This guy talks so easy. Me? I practice 'chicken or pasta' for ten minutes in my head. I need to fix this. My friend told me about this app Loora. You just talk to it. I'm trying it now. Speaker D: Hello, where are you flying? Speaker C: I fly London. Speaker D: Do you mean 'I am flying to London'? Speaker C: Yes, sorry. I am flying to London. Speaker D: Any luggage to check in? Speaker C: Luggage? Speaker D: Yes, luggage. It might be bags, suitcases and other containers people use to carry their belongings when traveling. Speaker C: Oh, I have one luggage. Sorry, I'm not so good English. Speaker D: Do you mean 'My English isn't that good'? No worries. How many times have you spoken in English over the past month? Speaker C: Actually I didn't. Speaker D: So how do you expect to speak fluently? Speaker C: I not have anyone to practice with. Speaker D: Just download Loora. We'll speak together daily and you'll be fluent in no time. Speaker C: That's amazing. I'm adding this to my daily routine today.

summary: A woman on a flight feels anxious about speaking English and decides to try the Loora app. The ad showcases a split-screen conversation where an AI avatar corrects her grammar mistakes in real-time. The AI encourages daily practice to build confidence and fluency.
